hypothesis._variant_note: v2 family member 2 of 5 (H2: defensive ballast). Adds risk_vol_60d (lower-is-better, w=0.7) to the 3-leg GARP recipe to shrink max_drawdown. Frame is moderate (TopK=100, 20d rebalance) — between H1 stretch and the rejected baseline.
hypothesis.thesis_statement: Pairing the 3-leg GARP composite with a low-volatility leg (risk_vol_60d, lower_is_better, weight 0.7) reduces max_drawdown below 32% while preserving rank_icir>=0.20 and meeting the 3.5x annual_turnover floor at TopK=100/rebalance=20d.
hypothesis.mechanism: The low-vol anomaly is the cheapest known DD-reduction tool in long-only A-share research (B-grade screening: rank_icir=-0.31 monotonic on risk_vol_60d). Adding it as a lower-is-better leg pushes the composite toward names with lower trailing 60-day return volatility, which historically have shallower drawdowns in 2018Q4-style growth-pullback regimes. Frame moves to 20d cadence + TopK=100 to also pull turnover under floor.

source.title: Growth-strategy v2 variant family — H2 defensive ballast

pre_registered_concerns.most_likely_failure_mode: High-growth A-share names are typically high-volatility (small/mid-cap with earnings surprise sensitivity). risk_vol_60d as a lower-is-better leg may inversely correlate with grow_opprofit_qoq and grow_roe_yoy at the cross-sectional rank level, neutralizing the composite. Symptom: rank_icir collapses from prior 0.466 toward 0.10-0.20. Still passes the 0.030 floor, but signal degradation may cancel the DD benefit.
pre_registered_concerns.weakest_assumption: weight=0.7 on risk_vol_60d is heuristic. Too low: defensive effect is washed out by GARP rank weight (3.0 total). Too high: signal degradation dominates. Cannot grid-search inside hypothesis_validation; this is a one-shot frozen weight commitment.
pre_registered_concerns.what_would_falsify_this: IS gate rejection on (max_drawdown > 0.32) means low-vol pairing is insufficient; H3's multi-component approach or H5's universe-filter combo are required. IS gate rejection on (rank_icir < 0.030) means the low-vol leg is destructive at this weight and needs to be removed or down-weighted.
pre_registered_concerns.priors_on_cost_sensitivity: 20d rebalance at TopK=100 should produce ~3-5x turnover similar to H1; cost drag ~60-100 bps. The defensive leg may slightly increase turnover if vol regimes shift quarterly more than fundamentals do.

prescription._note: v2 H2 defensive ballast: GARP x 3 (w=1) + risk_vol_60d (w=0.7, lower_is_better). TopK=100, rebalance=20d, max_pos=0.015 (feasibility: 0.015 * 100 = 1.5 >= 1.0).
